summary: This cluster synthesizes research on the taxonomic revision and systematic classification of diatoms in South Korea, specifically adopting Simonsen’s classification framework. The studies reorganized the diatom hierarchy into 2 orders, 5 suborders, 21 families, 5 sub-families, and 160 genera, encompassing 7,800 genera and 147 families globally. A key finding was the introduction of the Korean vernacular term “dolmal” (meaning “silicified algae”) for 147 genera, standardizing nomenclature within a revised classification system. The geographic scope is exclusively focused on South Korea, reflecting a national effort to align local diatom taxonomy with international systematic standards.
entity_sample: Pseudonitzschia Peragallo in H. Peragallo et M. Peragallo 1900
